name: Vinegar Taffy
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Vanilla (or other flavoring such as orange or lemon)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook sugar, water, corn syrup, salt, and vinegar in a saucepan until the mixture forms a hard ball when dropped into cold water (260°F on a candy thermometer).
2. Pour the hot mixture onto a greased dish or platter.
3. Sprinkle the chosen flavoring over the top of the cooled mixture.
4. Using only your fingertips, gently pull the outer edges of the candy toward the center until the mixture is cool and firm enough to handle.
5. Dab the tips of your fingers in cornstarch or fat (e.g., shortening) before pulling to prevent sticking.
6. Pull the taffy with both hands, folding it over and pulling out long strands repeatedly. The longer you pull and the cooler the mixture becomes, the firmer it will be.
7. Stop pulling when the taffy is difficult to pull out and feels quite firm.
8. Cut the cooled taffy into thin strips using greased scissors.
9. Roll each strip in powdered sugar or wrap in wax paper.
10. Store the finished taffy in an airtight container at room temperature.
This recipe, as found in Grandma Ruby's notes, is likely 30 to 40 years old, possibly more.
